key_points: A Constitution is a set of fundamental rules and principles according to which a country is governed
The Constitution defines the powers and responsibilities of the government
It protects the rights and freedoms of citizens
The Indian Constitution came into effect on 26 January 1950
The Constituent Assembly prepared the Constitution after India became independent
The Constitution ensures equality, justice and liberty for all citizens
examples: Citizens can go to court if their Fundamental Rights are violated
The Right to Equality ensures that everyone is treated equally before the law
The Right to Freedom allows citizens to express their opinions freely
Government institutions follow constitutional rules while making decisions
formulas: Constitution → Rules + Principles that guide the government
Justice and Equality → Protected through Fundamental Rights
exam_tips: Mention the year 1950 when writing about the Indian Constitution
Write examples of Fundamental Rights in answers
Explain how the Constitution protects citizens' rights
